#+title: Backlog index regeneration skips the discarded bucket
#+description: regenerate_backlog_indexes.py rebuilds inbox/next/deferred.org but not discarded.org, although discarded.org declares itself auto-generated by that script — so discarded captures are never indexed.
#+type: capture
#+level: cross
#+filetags: :agile:backlog:compass:tooling:codegen:
#+created: 2026-06-09
#+updated: 2026-06-09

This page is a [[id:671F18E4-E09C-4B3B-BD24-D33DF8AE38A6][capture]] in the *inbox* bucket of the product backlog — a pre-sprint idea, not yet pulled into a sprint as a story.

* What

=projects/ores.codegen/scripts/regenerate_backlog_indexes.py= walks only
=inbox/=, =next/=, and =deferred/= and rewrites those three index docs. It
never touches =discarded.org=, yet =discarded.org='s table carries the
"Auto-generated by =regenerate_backlog_indexes.py=. Do not edit this table by
hand." banner — so discarded captures are silently never indexed (found when
discarding two resolved timeline captures). Either add =discarded= to the
script's bucket list (with a header template, like the other three) or drop
the auto-generated banner from =discarded.org=.

* Why

The doc claims an index that the tool never produces, so the discarded bucket
is permanently stale and there is no safe way to refresh it (the banner says
not to hand-edit).

* References

- =projects/ores.codegen/scripts/regenerate_backlog_indexes.py= — walks
  inbox/next/deferred only.
- =doc/agile/product_backlog/discarded.org= — claims auto-generation.

* See also
